step_by_step_thinking: Condylar fractures involve disruptions around the condyles of the mandible, specifically impacting the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), potentially altering its functionality. Dysarthrosis refers to difficulties in producing clear speech sounds, suggesting involvement primarily in neuromuscular control pathways rather than purely structural issues like those addressed by the anatomical classifications here. Given the context, dysarthrosis would represent disturbances solely in function without necessarily implying any concurrent disturbance in the underlying anatomy if the condylar displacement did not further impact adjacent tissues adversely.
raw_response:
Condylar fractures involve disruptions around the condyles of the mandible, specifically impacting the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), potentially altering its functionality. Dysarthrosis refers to difficulties in producing clear speech sounds, suggesting involvement primarily in neuromuscular control pathways rather than purely structural issues like those addressed by the anatomical classifications here. Given the context, dysarthrosis would represent disturbances solely in function without necessarily implying any concurrent disturbance in the underlying anatomy if the condylar displacement did not further impact adjacent tissues adversely.
\boxed{C} Based on the discussion about dysarthrosis referring to difficulties in producing clear speech sounds and given the focus on function without specifying additional anatomical disturbances unless explicitly mentioned, option \( \boxed{\text{C}} \) best fits representing "Disturbance of function only." Options involving simultaneous disturbances in both anatomy and function do not align directly with defining dysarthrosis alone, making \(\boxed{\text{C}}\) the most appropriate response among the choices.
